encounterLog.description: Mist clings to the forest floor and a hush rides on the breeze — the kind of quiet that follows when every animal has gone still at once. To the south where the path opens, you can still see daylight; ahead, the canopy crowds together and a faint coppery smell drifts on the air. Somewhere deeper in, a low rhythmic chant rises and falls.

A narrow stair drops away beneath the broken flagstones of an old roadside chapel, and the air rising from below is colder than the night above. Tallow-smoke clings to your cloak as you descend. Three chambers lie ahead — antechamber, vestibule, and a deeper sanctum where lamplight flickers against carved stone. From somewhere within, the faintest thread of voices threads through the dark, rising and falling in a cadence that does not belong to any language you remember.

Mist clings to the forest floor and a hush rides on the breeze — the kind of quiet that follows when every animal has gone still at once. To the south where the path opens, you can still see daylight; ahead, the canopy crowds together and a faint coppery smell drifts on the air. Somewhere deeper in, a low rhythmic chant rises and falls.

You and a frightened traveller stand at the near end of a narrow stone bridge spanning a dark gorge. Two cloaked figures block the far end — armed, still, and watching. One steps forward and raises a hand. "Ten gold," he growls, "or you don't cross." The traveller edges close beside you and whispers: "I'm with you — whatever you decide."

A narrow stair drops away beneath the broken flagstones of an old roadside chapel, and the air rising from below is colder than the night above. Tallow-smoke clings to your cloak as you descend. Three chambers lie ahead — antechamber, vestibule, and a deeper sanctum where lamplight flickers against carved stone. From somewhere within, the faintest thread of voices threads through the dark, rising and falling in a cadence that does not belong to any language you remember.

A narrow stair drops away beneath the broken flagstones of an old roadside chapel, and the air rising from below is colder than the night above. Tallow-smoke clings to your cloak as you descend. Three chambers lie ahead — antechamber, vestibule, and a deeper sanctum where lamplight flickers against carved stone. From somewhere within, the faintest thread of voices threads through the dark, rising and falling in a cadence that does not belong to any language you remember.

storylog.narrative: The adventurer descended into the cold dark beneath the chapel, moving through the antechamber and vestibule without confrontation, until the sanctum opened around them with its carved walls and guttering lamplight. Nothing stirred, and the voices that had threaded up through the stone fell silent before they could be found.

The adventurer descended into the cold dark beneath the chapel, moving through the antechamber and vestibule without incident until the sanctum opened before them, its carved walls breathing faint lamplight and the low, rhythmic murmur of voices in no recognizable tongue. Nothing stirred to oppose the passage, and no hand was raised in threat or greeting. The flickering lamps burned on, indifferent, as the chanting continued somewhere just beyond the reach of the light.

The two cloaked figures held the far end of the bridge in silence, demanding payment for passage while the traveller pressed close and made clear they would follow whatever course was chosen. No coin changed hands and no blade was drawn — the standoff settled into stillness, the dark gorge yawning beneath the narrow span as both sides waited for the other to move first.

The mist-shrouded forest held its breath as the adventurer pressed deeper along the path, the canopy closing overhead and the faint copper tang of the air growing heavier with each step. Somewhere ahead, a low rhythmic chanting rose and fell through the stillness, unhurried and deliberate. Nothing moved, and nothing spoke — only the sound and the silence, and the daylight receding at the traveller's back.

The adventurer descended into the cold dark beneath the chapel, moving through the antechamber and vestibule without incident until the sanctum opened before them, its lamplight casting shifting shapes across the carved stone walls. No enemy stirred, no blade was drawn, and the strange cadence of voices faded into silence before its source could be found. Whatever had gathered here had already gone — or was waiting still, somewhere beyond the reach of the light.

The mist-laden forest held its breath as the adventurer moved forward along the shadowed path, the coppery tang in the air growing heavier with each step toward the crowding canopy. Somewhere ahead, the rhythmic chant continued its rise and fall, unhurried and indifferent to any approach. Nothing stirred — no blade was drawn, no word spoken — and the daylight at the southern end of the path grew no closer.
